rxn_class_summary: Nickel-catalyzed electrochemical three-component 1,2-dicarbofunctionalization of styrenes with aryl iodides and 3-iodooxetane via a radical pathway
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction generates Ni(0)Ln from Ni(II); the iron anode is sacrificially oxidized (Fe -> Fe(n+) + ne-); alkyl iodide (3-iodooxetane) undergoes SET to form alkyl radical that adds to styrene

key_intermediates.name: Ni(II)-I (G) reduced back to Ni(0) at cathode

electron_flow: Cathode reduces Ni(II) to Ni(0), which oxidatively adds ArI to give Ar-Ni(II)-I; this Ni species reduces the alkyl iodide via SET to release an alkyl radical that adds to styrene, generating a benzylic radical that recombines with Ar-Ni(II) to give a Ni(III) intermediate undergoing reductive elimination; the iron anode is sacrificially oxidized to balance charge.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Target product 4a fully suppressed (0%); TEMPO-oxetanyl adduct 7 isolated in 13% with full system and 31% with only 3a, confirming alkyl radical formation and suggesting Ni(I) generation by disproportionation

probe_reagents.name: DMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Used as spin trap for EPR detection of radical intermediates from 1a and 2a under electrolysis

notes: Stoichiometric reaction without electric current using Ni(cod)2 still afforded product 4a (10%) and isomer 4a' (40%), supporting feasibility of the Ni(0)/Ni(II)/Ni(III) cycle independent of continuous current. TEMPO trapping with only 3a indicates Ni(I) may form via disproportionation pathway alongside the main cycle.
